ocr.text: The first table presents socio-demographic variables among paediatric and psychiatric nurses, detailing gender, age, marital
status, religion, working experience, and previous encounter. For paediatric nurses, gender distribution shows 201 males
(15.4%), 101 females (84.6%), totaling 130 (100.0%), while psychiatric nurses exhibit 316 males (33.3%), 293 females
(66.7%), totaling 609 (100.0%). Age categorization reveals that 65 (50.0%) of paediatric nurses are under 30, 52 (34.6%) are
aged 30-39, and 13 (15.4%) are over 39, totaling 130 (100.0%), whereas psychiatric nurses consist of 44 (47.3%) under 30, 51
(37.6%) aged 30-39, and 19 (15.1%) over 39, totaling 93 (100.0%). Marital status indicates 45 (34.6%) single and 85 (65.4%)
married among paediatric nurses, while psychiatric nurses show 40 (43.0%) single and 53 (57.0%) married. In terms of religion,
110 (84.6%) paediatric nurses identify as Christians, compared to 84 (90.3%) psychiatric nurses. Working experience data
shows 52 (40.0%) with less than 5 years, 52 (42.3%) with 5-10 years, and 17 (17.7%) over 10 years in paediatric nurses, while
psychiatric nurses report 40 (43.0%), 39 (43.0%), and 14 (14.0%) respectively. Previous encounters reveal 76 (58.5%) of
paediatric nurses had previous experiences compared to 57 (61.3%) of psychiatric nurses. The second table compares CEA
levels and overall survival months between PPTR and non-PPTR groups, with noted values across four CEA ranges: 0–5
